commands.repair_reason: The remaining blocker is maintainer/security approval of the SSRF boundary and SDK surface; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.; Cleared: Security-sensitive SSRF and SDK diff reviewed; I found no concrete SSRF bypass or supply-chain regression, but policy approval remains required. (sha=968c2e42350ff494ba8eeca073cb27160d0a4244)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds exact-origin SSRF trust for custom/local model provider baseUrl requests, exports allowed-origin SSRF helpers through the plugin SDK runtime, updates provider docs/config help, and adds SSRF/provider regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main reaches guarded provider fetch with fake-IP or broad allowPrivateNetwork policy only, so a configured private custom/local baseUrl lacks exact-origin trust. The linked issue also provides concrete config, logs, and curl sanity evidence.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is maintainer/security approval of the SSRF boundary and SDK surface; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.
